description_long: This house complex is built into a sloped terrain, featuring multiple interconnected rectangular structures with flat roofs and large window panels. The buildings are arranged in tiers, following the contour of the landscape, and are surrounded by green areas that mimic natural grass. The exterior walls are primarily light-colored, with some sections clad in darker materials, possibly stone or brick, for accent. The lower level includes a long, covered driveway or garage area, with direct access to the main road. The design emphasizes modern architectural elements, such as clean lines, open terraces, and expansive glass surfaces, providing ample natural light and outdoor living spaces. The overall layout suggests a multi-family or luxury residential development with private and communal zones.
description: A modern, multi-level house complex with flat roofs, large windows, and terraced landscaping built into a hillside. The design features clean lines, open terraces, and a mix of light and dark exterior materials.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry, terraced layout, and covered driveway or garage beneath the upper living spaces.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stepped arrangement of the buildings, integration with the sloped terrain, and multiple levels of outdoor terraces.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the retaining walls, green roof sections, and the back facades of the uppermost structures.
description_view_3: Opposite side view revealing the depth of the complex, additional access points, and the continuation of the sloped landscaping.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large picture windows and glass panels
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a flat overhang, wide steps, and large glass doors, with minimalistic detailing and direct access from the driveway.
